name: Bittersweet/White Chocolate Bar Cookies/pt 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Grease several baking sheets and set aside.
3. In a large bowl, stir together flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, combine egg, almond paste, and almond extract. Beat with an electric mixer on low speed for about 3 minutes, or until the almond paste is thoroughly incorporated and smooth.
5. Add butter to the mixture and continue beating until it is lightened.
6. Beat in sugar until well blended and smooth.
7. Gradually beat in the dry ingredients and grated chocolate until thoroughly incorporated.
8. Divide dough into two portions.
9. Shape each portion into a smooth, evenly thick 13-inch log.
10. Press down each log to form bars that are approximately 2.25 inches wide.
11. Trim off and discard the ends of the bars.
12. Mark the bars and cut each log crosswise into 0.5 inch wide strips.
13. Place the strips upright on baking sheets, spacing them about 1.25 inches apart and flattening them slightly so they don't fall over as they bake.
14. Place the baking sheets in the center of the oven and bake for 11 to 13 minutes, or until the tops are almost firm in the center. Remove from the oven and let stand for 3 to 4 minutes.
15. Carefully transfer the cookies to wire racks and let them cool completely.
16. To prepare dark chocolate glaze, combine shortening and bittersweet and unsweetened chocolates in the top of a double boiler over barely simmering water. Heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is melted and smooth. Remove from heat.
17. Dip the tops of the cookies one at a time into the glaze. It should cover their tops and sides.
18. Place glazed bars on wire racks set over sheets of waxed paper and let stand until the glaze sets.
19. To prepare white chocolate glaze, combine shortening and white chocolate in a small, heavy saucepan over lowest heat. Warm, stirring frequently, until the chocolate is smooth and fluid.
20. Once the dark chocolate glaze on the cookies is completely set, drizzle diagonal lines of the white chocolate glaze across the tops of the bars (Piping the glaze through a pastry bag fitted with a fine writing tip will give the most professional finish, but drizzling the glaze from a spoon will look fine too).
21. Let the bars stand until the chocolate sets.
Store the bars in the refrigerator for up to 2 or 3 days. The cookies may be frozen unglazed and then glazed after thawing. Once glazed, they should be kept refrigerated. Makes about 40 2.25 inch bar cookies.
